title: Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels, Belgium | 12-Month European Policy Internship
summary: Microsoft has officially opened applications for its highly prestigious Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 based in Brussels, Belgium. This exciting opportunity is designed for students and early-career professionals who are passionate about European politics, technology policy, cloud infrastructure, digital sovereignty, and international governance. The internship will begin in August or September 2026 and will run for a duration of 12 months, offering selected candidates the chance to work directly with Microsoft’s European Government Affairs team in Brussels. This internship is an exceptional opportunity for aspiring policy professionals, public affairs specialists, international relations graduates, and technology policy enthusiasts who want to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading technology companies. About the Microsoft Government Affairs Internship 2026 Microsoft is recruiting a Cloud, Infrastructure Tech Sovereignty Policy Coordinator Intern to support its European Government Affairs Office in Brussels. The role will involve working closely with senior government affairs professionals and contributing to Microsoft’s engagement with European institutions and stakeholders. The internship is supervised under the leadership of Nanna-Louise Linde, Microsoft’s Vice President for European Government Affairs. Microsoft’s European Government Affairs organization focuses on strengthening relationships and dialogue with European policymakers and institutions while supporting Europe’s broader digital transformation agenda. Interns will gain direct exposure to: European Union policymaking Cloud and AI governance Technology sovereignty discussions Digital infrastructure policy Telecom and subsea cable policy Quantum computing policy Corporate public affairs strategy Executive stakeholder engagement This is a high-impact internship where interns will actively contribute to policy analysis, stakeholder coordination, strategic planning, event management, and executive engagement preparation. Internship Location Brussels Region, Belgium Internship Duration 12 Months Internship Start Date August 2026 or September 2026 Work Model Hybrid work arrangement 3 days per week in-office Employment Type Internship Professional Area Corporate Affairs Government Affairs Travel Requirements Less than 25% Key Responsibilities Selected interns will support Microsoft’s European Government Affairs Office through a broad range of responsibilities related to policy coordination, stakeholder engagement, and strategic communications. 1. Political, Policy, and Regulatory Analysis Interns will: Monitor emerging European technology policy developments Analyze public policy issues related to cloud infrastructure and AI Support policy research on sovereign cloud technologies Study developments in telecom and subsea cable infrastructure Track discussions on quantum computing policy Coordinate contributions from internal stakeholders Prepare summaries and policy analyses for internal teams 2. Strategic Planning and Stakeholder Engagement Interns will: Conduct stakeholder mapping exercises Build engagement strategies with EU institutions Support outreach to policymakers and regulators Help identify collaboration opportunities across Europe Assist in planning government affairs initiatives Contribute to internal policy strategy development 3. Project Management and Event Coordination Participants will: Assist in organizing high-level political engagements Coordinate executive visits and meetings in Brussels Support logistics for conferences and policy events Work with European institutions, think tanks, and trade associations Help manage cross-functional event planning activities 4. Research and Writing Interns will: Draft briefing notes and policy documents Support campaign-related research Develop executive talking points Assist with conference preparation materials Edit presentations and strategic communication documents Prepare internal policy decks and engagement materials 5. External Representation Interns may also: Attend external policy events and meetings Represent Microsoft in relevant discussions Provide detailed event summaries and analysis reports Qualifications and Eligibility Requirements Microsoft is seeking highly motivated and analytical candidates with a strong interest in technology policy and European governance. Applicants should possess: Educational Background A degree or academic background in: Law Political Science International Relations Philosophy Social Sciences Related disciplines Preferred Experience Previous experience through: Internships Public affairs consultancies EU institutions Policy organizations Corporate affairs teams Government-related environments Required Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Excellent analytical abilities Strong communication skills Project management experience Organizational and planning skills Attention to detail Networking abilities Team collaboration skills Adaptability in fast-paced environments Additional Requirements Applicants should also have: Strong interest in European and international politics Passion for technology and digital policy Fluency in spoken and written English Ability to manage competing deadlines Positive and collaborative mindset Knowledge of additional European languages will be considered an advantage. Benefits of the Internship The Microsoft Government Affairs Internship provides a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in international technology policy and corporate public affairs. Benefits include: Experience working at Microsoft Exposure to European policymaking Networking with EU stakeholders and policymakers Participation in high-level political engagements Professional mentorship International work environment Hands-on public affairs experience Opportunity to contribute to technology governance discussions Career development in government affairs and policy strategy Why This Opportunity Stands Out This internship is particularly valuable because it combines: Technology policy International governance Public affairs Corporate strategy Political engagement European Union policymaking Interns will work at the intersection of politics, business, and technology during a period of rapid transformation in Europe’s digital landscape. For students and graduates interested in global policy careers, digital governance, AI regulation, or international public affairs, this internship offers world-class exposure and professional growth opportunities. Application Deadline Microsoft stated that: The position will remain open for a minimum of 5 days. Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible. Final Thoughts The Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels present a remarkable opportunity for ambitious young professionals seeking careers in international policy, corporate affairs, digital governance, and European technology regulation. By joining Microsoft’s European Government Affairs team, interns will gain direct experience shaping conversations around cloud sovereignty, AI infrastructure, telecom policy, and Europe’s evolving digital future. For candidates passionate about technology, politics, innovation, and global impact, this internship could become a major stepping stone toward an international policy career. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels, Belgium | 12-Month European Policy Internship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Yale University Scholarships 2026-27(Fully-funded for Undergraduate, Masters and PhD)
summary: Start your study adventure in USA. Good news! Yale University Scholarships are currently open. In this article we will explain in detail about this scholarship, its benefits and step by step application process. Yale University Scholarship is a fully funded scholarship for international students. This scholarship is offered for undergraduate, masters and PhD. Yale Scholarship can vary from a few hundred dollars to over $70,000 per year; the average Yale need-based scholarship is over $50,000. This university is one of the greatest universities in the USA and is part of the Ivy League schools. The Ivy League schools are synonymous with prestige and academic excellence. The dream of many high school students is to study at Ivy League schools because professional success is almost guaranteed by studying at these universities. Yale University is the third oldest university in the US and was found in 1701 at New Haven, Connecticut. It was originally founded as a school to educate the clergy but soon was changed to teach many other fields of study. Many well-known students have graduated from Yale including Nobel Laureates, Supreme Court Justices, US Presidents and Rhodes Scholars. This university is ranked among the top universities in the world because of its academic brilliance. This university stands at 15th in the world by top Universities and 3rd in the US by the US News because of its prestige and an excellent academic standard. Yale University offer a number of scholarships in the form of financial aid. Approximately 63% of undergraduate students, including international, received financial aid. For graduate scholarships, students have to apply to the departments of their choice because funding policies are different for each department. Graduate funding is determined by individual departments, not the university. For PhD. students, fully funded scholarships are very common. Every student at least receive scholarship which covers full tuition scholarship, hospitalization fellowship, and an annual stipend. Scholarship Summary Level of Study: Undergraduate, Masters, PhD Institution(s): Yale University Study in: USA Opportunity Focus Areas: To view the full list of undergraduate majors click here . To view the full list of graduate majors click here . Program Period: Program period depends on the chosen Program from 2 to 5 Academic Years. Deadline: Click here for undergraduate deadlines / Click here for graduate deadlines Scholarship Coverage Yale University scholarship is awarded to cover 100% of the financial need demonstrated by the family. They take into account your parent’s income, assets, and finances to determine your scholarship amount. The Yale Scholarship can vary from a few hundred dollars to over $70,000 per year; the average Yale need-based scholarship is over $50,000. Also, students whose parents earn less than 65,000$ a year do not pay anything! So if your parents earn less than 65,000$ a year, Yale University scholarship covers tuition, room, and board! Eligibility Criteria for Yale University Scholarships To be eligible for Yale University Scholarships, following criteria is to be fulfilled: Required Language: English. TOEFL scores of at least the following: 100 on the internet-based TOEFL 600 on the paper-based TOEFL 250 on the computer-based TOEFL IELTS scores of 7 or higher and Pearson Test scores of 70 or higher. For more information, check here . Eligible Countries: All World Countries. You are enrolled in or eligible to participate in the Federal Free or Reduced Price Lunch program (FRPL). You have received or are eligible to receive an ACT or SAT testing fee waiver. You are enrolled in a federal, state, or local program that aids students from low-income families (e.g., TRIO programs such as Upward Bound). You are a ward of the state or an orphan. Your annual family income falls within the Income Eligibility Guidelines set by the USDA Food and Nutrition Service. You live in a federally subsidized public housing, a foster home or are homeless. Your family receives public assistance. You can provide a supporting statement from a school official, college access counselor, financial aid officer, or community leader. How to Apply for Yale University Scholarship? Please follow the following steps to avail Yale University Scholarship: Check these specific requirements of this scholarship: Two Teacher Recommendations $80 Application Fee or Fee Waiver Standardized Testing Results Counselor Recommendation and School Report (including transcript) Final Report Mid-Year Report For Undergraduate students, click here to apply. For graduate students. Please apply through choosing the appropriate program from here. You will get an e-mail for acceptance or refuse. Website To know more about Yale University Scholarship, please visit official website: Official Website The post Yale University Scholarships 2026-27(Fully-funded for Undergraduate, Masters and PhD) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate Position 2026: Apply for a Three-Year Research, Education, and Training Role at MIT
summary: The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab, widely known as J-PAL, is inviting applications for the Senior Training Associate position at J-PAL Global . This opportunity is based at the J-PAL Global office at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States . This is a strong opportunity for candidates with a background in economics, political science, education, international development, program evaluation, quantitative research, randomized evaluations, or related fields. The role is designed for individuals who are passionate about poverty alleviation, evidence-informed policymaking, rigorous impact evaluation, and global development research. The successful candidate will join J-PAL’s Research, Education, and Training team , supporting the design, planning, delivery, and evaluation of training programs for policymakers, practitioners, researchers, governments, NGOs, foundations, universities, and development organizations around the world. About J-PAL Global The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab is a global research center working to reduce poverty by ensuring that public policy is shaped by scientific evidence. J-PAL is anchored by a network of more than 1,100 leading researchers based at universities across the world. Headquartered at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology , J-PAL works across multiple global offices and has more than 500 staff members supporting research, policy engagement, education, training, and partnerships. J-PAL’s work focuses on using rigorous research, especially randomized evaluations, to understand what works in reducing poverty and improving lives. Through its partnerships with governments, non-governmental organizations, foundations, multilateral institutions, and universities, J-PAL helps turn evidence into policy action. Position Overview J-PAL Global is recruiting a Senior Training Associate to support its mission of promoting evidence-informed policy. The position will be based at the J-PAL Global office at MIT in Cambridge, Massachusetts. The role is part of J-PAL’s Research, Education, and Training team , also known as the RET team. This team works in a fast-paced and collaborative environment to equip policymakers, researchers, practitioners, and staff with the knowledge and tools needed to conduct and use rigorous impact evaluations. The Senior Training Associate will contribute to J-PAL’s global training work by developing new teaching materials, supporting online and in-person courses, assisting with workshops, and strengthening learning resources on randomized evaluations and cost-effectiveness. The role also includes working with learners and partners from around the world, creating open-access resources for J-PAL’s online courses, and developing tailored training materials for partner organizations. Key Details of the Opportunity Position title: Senior Training Associate Organization: J-PAL Global Host institution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology Location: Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States Earliest start date: 1 July 2026 Length of commitment: Three years or more Application deadline: June 15 Application review: Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis Work format: Hybrid, with at least two in-office days per week Visa sponsorship: Not available Why This Role Matters This position is ideal for someone who wants to contribute to global development through research, training, and evidence-based policy. Many governments and development organizations want to solve urgent problems related to poverty, education, health, labor markets, social protection, agriculture, governance, and economic opportunity. However, effective solutions require strong evidence. J-PAL’s training work helps policymakers, researchers, and development practitioners understand how to generate, interpret, and use evidence responsibly. As a Senior Training Associate, the selected candidate will help build the capacity of people and institutions working on real-world policy challenges. The role combines research communication, teaching, data analysis, training design, partnership support, and global development engagement. Main Responsibilities The Senior Training Associate will contribute to several areas of J-PAL’s training and education work. Responsibilities may include the following: 1. Developing Teaching Materials The selected candidate will research and design new teaching modules for J-PAL’s training programs. These materials may cover topics such as randomized evaluations, impact evaluation methods, cost-effectiveness analysis, data use, and evidence-informed decision-making. This may involve preparing: Presentations Case studies Training manuals Course exercises Learning guides Technical explanations Teaching resources for online and in-person learners 2. Supporting Online and In-Person Training The Senior Training Associate will serve as a teaching assistant for J-PAL’s online and in-person training offerings. This includes helping learners strengthen their ability to generate and use rigorous evidence. The role may involve supporting learners during courses, explaining technical concepts, assisting with assignments, answering questions, and helping participants understand randomized evaluation methods. 3. Planning and Delivering Workshops The position involves assisting with the planning, organization, and delivery of training courses and workshops for key partners around the world. These partners may include: Government agencies Non-governmental organizations Foundations Universities Multilateral aid agencies Research institutions Development practitioners The selected candidate may also have opportunities to travel domestically and internationally to support or participate in training courses. 4. Collaborating Across J-PAL Offices J-PAL operates through a global network of regional offices. The Senior Training Associate will coordinate and collaborate with training colleagues across these offices. This may include contributing to knowledge management, improving training processes, organizing shared resources, and helping ensure consistency and quality across J-PAL’s training materials. 5. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Data Analysis The role includes maintaining tracking, monitoring, and evaluation systems for J-PAL’s courses. The selected candidate will help analyze data to assess learning outcomes, participant engagement, and course effectiveness. This makes the role suitable for candidates who are comfortable with quantitative data analysis and interested in improving educational and training programs through evidence. 6. Supporting Partner Relationships The Senior Training Associate will also support the development of relationships with key partners in governments, NGOs, foundations, universities, multilateral aid agencies, and other institutions around the world. This aspect of the role requires strong communication skills, cultural awareness, professionalism, and an interest in working across different institutional and global contexts. Required Qualifications Applicants should meet the following educational, technical, and professional requirements. Educational Background A bachelor’s degree is required in one of the following fields: Economics Political science Education International development A related field A master’s degree is preferred , but not strictly required. Applicants must have training in and a strong technical understanding of: Econometrics Randomized evaluations Quantitative research methods Impact evaluation methods Experience Requirements J-PAL prefers candidates with around four years of relevant work experience . This experience may include work gained as a student. Relevant experience may include: Program evaluation Quantitative data analysis Economics research Development research Education or training work Impact evaluation Policy research Research assistance Data management Related analytical roles Applicants with fewer than four years of experience are also welcome to apply. Exceptional candidates with less than four years of experience may be considered for an Associate-level position . Skills and Competencies Required Strong Interest in Poverty Alleviation Candidates should have a strong interest in poverty alleviation policy and research. This interest may be demonstrated through coursework, research, study abroad, work abroad, development experience, or professional exposure to policy and social impact work. Quantitative Skills Applicants must have knowledge of: Data management Data analysis Econometrics Quantitative research methods These skills are important because the role involves working with training materials related to randomized evaluations, evidence generation, and policy-relevant research. Software Skills Applicants must have a good command of at least one of the following: Stata R Python Candidates should also be comfortable using Google Workspace tools, including: Google Docs Google Sheets Google Slides Google Forms Additional skills that are considered a plus include: LaTeX GitHub Other coding languages Thoughtful use of AI tools to streamline workflows Cultural Competencies J-PAL is looking for applicants who are comfortable working in a diverse and multicultural environment. The role involves collaboration with colleagues, learners, partners, policymakers, and institutions from different parts of the world. Applicants should demonstrate sensitivity and adaptability across: Culture Gender Religion Race Nationality Age Professional background J-PAL encourages diversity, equity, and inclusion in its work and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds. The organization especially encourages people who are underrepresented in economics to apply. Location and Work Arrangement This position is based in Cambridge, Massachusetts , at J-PAL Global’s office at MIT. The role follows a hybrid work arrangement. Staff are currently expected to work in the office at least two days per week , specifically Tuesdays and Wednesdays. Remote work is allowed on the remaining three days. However, hybrid work policies may change annually. The position is not eligible for full-time remote work. Visa Sponsorship Visa sponsorship is not available for this position. Candidates must already be authorized to work in the United States for a minimum of three years. This is an important requirement, and applicants should confirm their work authorization before applying. Compensation and Benefits J-PAL states that pay range and pay grade information for this role is available through the relevant MIT compensation information page. As a member of the MIT community, the successful candidate will receive a competitive compensation package for the non-profit international development field. Benefits may include: Comprehensive health insurance 401(k) match Pension-based retirement plan Tuition assistance Commuter benefits MIT community discounts Generous vacation policy Additional MIT benefits J-PAL staff members may also be eligible to take proctored exams in the Data, Economics, and Design of Policy MicroMasters courses at no cost. These course certificates can count toward the DEDP MicroMasters credential. Application Process Interested candidates must apply through the MIT human resources system . Applicants should search for MIT Job ID: 25712 and complete the application on the MIT portal. The application requires candidates to upload a single PDF document into the “Resume” field. This single PDF should include: Cover letter The cover letter should explain why the applicant is interested in the position at J-PAL Global and why they are qualified for the role. Resume or CV The resume or CV should clearly show relevant education, work experience, technical skills, research experience, and training-related experience. Transcripts for all previous university-level coursework Unofficial transcripts are accepted. Applicants should only apply to the J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate position, MIT Job ID 25712 . Selection Process All applicants will be asked to complete a short data task as part of the screening process. Shortlisted applicants will then be asked to complete a short technical screening interview via Zoom. Candidates who advance further will be invited to additional Zoom interviews. Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the June 15 deadline. Why You Should Apply This opportunity is suitable for candidates who want to combine research, education, training, and global development impact. It is especially relevant for people who enjoy translating technical research concepts into practical learning materials for policymakers, practitioners, and researchers. You should consider applying if you: Are passionate about poverty reduction and evidence-based policy Have a background in economics, education, political science, international development, or related fields Understand econometrics, randomized evaluations, or quantitative research methods Enjoy developing teaching materials and training resources Have experience in data analysis or program evaluation Want to work with global partners in government, NGOs, foundations, universities, and multilateral agencies Are comfortable working in a multicultural environment Are authorized to work in the United States for at least three years Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate position is June 15 . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so applicants should submit their materials as early as possible. How to Apply To apply, candidates should visit the MIT human resources system, search for MIT Job ID 25712 , and submit the required application materials. Applicants must upload one PDF document containing their cover letter, resume or CV, and transcripts. APPLY HERE. For more information about this opportunity click here. Discover more job opportunities on OFY here. The post J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate Position 2026: Apply for a Three-Year Research, Education, and Training Role at MIT appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year
summary: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme 2026 sponsored by the Republic of Korea. This highly competitive international opportunity offers young Korean professionals the chance to work at the ICC headquarters in The Hague, Netherlands, while contributing to global justice and international institutional management. The position is hosted within the Human Resources Section of the ICC Registry and provides selected candidates with hands-on experience in strategic HR management, organizational development, policy implementation, workforce planning, recruitment operations, learning and development, and international administration. The application deadline is 26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time). About the International Criminal Court (ICC) The International Criminal Court is one of the world’s leading international judicial institutions, responsible for investigating and prosecuting individuals accused of genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, and crimes of aggression. Headquartered in The Hague, Netherlands, the ICC operates with professionals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. The organization provides an international working environment focused on justice, accountability, professionalism, diversity, and global cooperation. The Human Resources Section plays a critical role in supporting the Court’s global mission by ensuring effective workforce management, organizational efficiency, strategic talent development, and compliance with international administrative standards. Overview of the JPO Position Position Title Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Duty Station The Hague, Netherlands Organization International Criminal Court (ICC) Organizational Unit Human Resources Section, Division of Management Services, Registry Contract Duration One-year appointment with possibility of extension according to the JPO Memorandum of Understanding Annual Salary Minimum Net Annual Salary (including post adjustment): €71,173 Application Deadline 26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time) What Makes This Opportunity Significant? This JPO role offers early-career professionals the rare opportunity to: Work directly within a major international organization Gain exposure to UN Common System HR frameworks Participate in strategic HR and organizational development projects Build expertise in international recruitment and policy implementation Contribute to organizational transformation initiatives Develop experience in multicultural and multinational workplace management Work alongside global experts in administration, HR operations, and institutional governance For candidates interested in long-term careers with the United Nations system, international NGOs, global institutions, or diplomatic organizations, this opportunity provides outstanding career-building exposure. Human Resources Section at the ICC The Human Resources Section provides centralized HR advisory, administrative, and strategic support across the Court. It operates within the Division of Management Services alongside: Budget Section Finance Section Safety and Security Section General Services Section Occupational Health Unit SAP Competence Centre The HR Section focuses on both operational and strategic human resource management functions. Key Areas Managed by the HR Section The section oversees: Recruitment and onboarding Workforce planning Job design and classification Payroll administration Employee benefits and entitlements Pension and insurance management Performance management Organizational development Learning and development programmes Career planning and succession management Staff relations and grievance support Structure of the Human Resources Section The HR Section consists of two specialized units: 1. HR Operations Unit This unit handles operational HR functions including: Recruitment processes Staff onboarding Compensation administration Payroll management Benefits and entitlements Post management Health insurance administration Pension administration HR-related staff complaints and grievances Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) staff data management 2. HR Organizational Development Unit This unit focuses on strategic HR initiatives such as: Workforce planning Talent management Organizational design Staff development Training programmes Learning and development strategies Performance management systems Leadership and managerial development Duties and Responsibilities Under the supervision of the Head of the Human Resources Operations Unit, the selected JPO will support multiple HR functions and strategic projects. Main Responsibilities Include: Supporting implementation of HR-related projects and strategic initiatives Conducting research on HR policies, procedures, and best practices from comparable international organizations Assisting in the development of HR guidelines and institutional procedures Liaising with HR specialists on recruitment, entitlements, insurance, policy interpretation, and administrative matters Reviewing HR issues and presenting analytical recommendations to management Supporting the monitoring and evaluation of HR policies and organizational practices Assisting in the development of HR performance indicators and management tools Contributing to Service Level Agreements and HR work plan tracking systems Conducting preliminary policy analysis and assisting in drafting position papers Designing internal communication materials supporting HR objectives and institutional goals Providing support across HR units whenever required Performing additional duties assigned by HR leadership Required Qualifications Educational Requirements Applicants must possess either: An advanced university degree in: Human Resources Management Business Administration Public Administration Law Social Sciences Information Management Or another related field OR A first-level university degree combined with two additional years of qualifying experience Professional Experience Requirements Candidates must have: Minimum two years of relevant HR experience OR Four years of experience if applying with a first-level university degree Desirable Experience Additional advantages include experience in: International organizations UN Common System salary and allowance structures Broad HR operations and administration HR policy implementation Organizational development initiatives Required Skills and Competencies The ICC seeks candidates with strong technical and interpersonal competencies. Important Skills Include: Human resources management knowledge Strong analytical and research skills Strategic planning capabilities Results-oriented work approach Ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines Excellent communication skills Professional writing ability Cross-cultural collaboration skills Confidentiality and discretion Flexibility and motivation Strong professional integrity Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in English or French Knowledge of the second working language is considered desirable. Additional knowledge of official ICC languages such as: Arabic Chinese Russian Spanish will be considered an advantage. Eligibility Requirements for Republic of Korea JPO Applicants This position is specifically sponsored by the Republic of Korea and therefore includes additional eligibility conditions. Applicants must: Be Korean citizens Be fluent in Korean and English Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree Meet English proficiency requirements Be under 32 years old as of 31 December 2026 Have completed or be exempt from Korean military service (for male applicants) Accepted English Test Scores Applicants must provide valid English test results meeting at least one of the following minimum scores: TOEFL iBT: 100 TEPS: 430 IELTS: 7 TOEIC: 900 Additional notes: TOEFL Home Edition is accepted TOEFL MyBest Scores are not accepted IELTS Academic and General Training are both accepted Scores remain valid for two years after the test date Military Service Age Extension The maximum age limit may be extended for applicants who completed Korean military service: One-year extension for service under one year Two-year extension for service between one and two years Three-year extension for service of two years or more Required Documents Applicants must submit all documents in English through the ICC e-recruitment system. Required Documents Include: Resident Register issued by the Korean Government University degree certificates English proficiency certificate Employment certificates detailing: Employment period Organization Department Position title Duties and responsibilities Military service certificate (if applicable) National Basic Livelihood Security certificate (for low-income applicants) Disability certificate (for applicants with disabilities) Why You Should Apply This ICC JPO opportunity offers substantial professional and international career benefits, including: International HR experience Exposure to global institutional operations High-level policy and organizational development work Competitive international salary Multicultural professional environment Networking with global professionals Career pathway into international organizations and the UN system Professional growth in strategic HR management Final Thoughts The ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) JPO Programme 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious Korean professionals seeking international exposure in human resources management and organizational development. Working at the International Criminal Court provides not only a competitive international career pathway but also the opportunity to contribute to one of the world’s most significant global justice institutions. Candidates with strong HR expertise, analytical capabilities, international outlook, and commitment to public service are strongly encouraged to apply before the 26 June 2026 deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the ICC Associate Human Resources Officer job opportunity. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . title: Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services
summary: Applications are now officially open for the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 by Bombshell Impact Design Agency, an award-winning social impact branding and design agency recognized as the Best Social Impact Branding Design Agency 2026 by the Corporate Vision Canadian Business Awards. This global initiative is designed to empower social impact organizations that are creating meaningful change in communities around the world. Through this grant, selected organizations will receive up to $10,000 USD worth of professional design, marketing, branding, and AI-powered business support services to help amplify their mission and increase visibility. The application deadline is August 19, 2026, in recognition of World Humanitarian Day. About the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant The “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant is more than a funding opportunity. It is a visibility and growth initiative created specifically for organizations that are driving social transformation but may lack the branding, communication, or digital infrastructure needed to expand their reach and impact. Bombshell Impact Design Agency believes that many world-changing missions remain unseen not because of lack of value, but because of limited visibility, storytelling, and strategic positioning. This initiative aims to bridge that gap by helping impactful organizations strengthen how they communicate and present their mission to the world. The program welcomes applications from organizations across every region globally, reinforcing the agency’s commitment to supporting changemakers regardless of geographic location. Grant Benefits Selected recipients can receive up to $10,000 USD in professional services, including: Branding and identity development Strategic marketing support AI-powered creative and communication services Digital visibility enhancement Storytelling and campaign support Design solutions tailored to mission-driven organizations Strategic impact communication support These services are intended to help organizations improve outreach, attract supporters, strengthen campaigns, and scale their impact more effectively. Why This Opportunity Matters Many social impact organizations struggle with limited resources for branding, communication, and digital marketing. While their missions may be transformative, they often face challenges in: Building public awareness Reaching donors and supporters Creating compelling digital campaigns Developing strong visual identities Competing for visibility online Communicating measurable impact effectively This grant directly addresses those challenges by providing expert-level creative and strategic services that many organizations would otherwise struggle to afford. Who Can Apply? The opportunity is open to social impact organizations worldwide that are actively working to create positive change in areas such as: Sustainability Environmental protection Community development Humanitarian support Education Accessibility Social justice Health and wellbeing Youth empowerment Innovation for social good Organizations of different sizes and stages are encouraged to apply, especially those with compelling missions and measurable impact stories. How to Apply Applicants must complete a simple multi-step application process. Step 1: Complete the Application Form Organizations are required to provide information about: Their mission Their organization The impact they are creating Their long-term goals The communities they serve Applicants should clearly explain how the grant support would help expand or strengthen their work. Step 2: Upload a Short Video Applicants must submit a 30–60 second video filmed on a phone or simple camera setup. The video should: Introduce the organization Share the mission and impact story Explain why the work matters Highlight the organization’s vision for change The agency specifically encourages applicants to share their story authentically and in their own voice. Step 3: Application Review and Winner Selection After the deadline closes, all applications will be reviewed carefully by the Bombshell Impact Design Agency team. Selected winners will then be announced following the evaluation process. Important Deadline Application Deadline: August 19, 2026 The deadline coincides with World Humanitarian Day, emphasizing the global importance of humanitarian and social impact initiatives. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare a compelling story-driven application. A Global Opportunity for Changemakers One of the strongest aspects of this initiative is its international accessibility. Organizations from every continent are eligible to apply, making this a truly global social impact opportunity. Whether an organization is based in Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Oceania, or the Middle East, they are encouraged to submit their application if they are making meaningful contributions toward positive change. The Core Message Behind the Initiative The campaign carries a powerful message: “The sky was never the limit. Visibility was.” This reflects the idea that many organizations already possess the passion, innovation, and commitment needed to change the world. What they often need most is strategic visibility and stronger communication support to reach larger audiences and unlock greater opportunities. Why Organizations Should Apply This grant presents a unique opportunity for social impact organizations to gain access to high-value professional services without direct financial cost. Organizations should consider applying because it offers: International visibility Access to expert branding support Professional marketing services AI-driven creative solutions Increased awareness opportunities Stronger communication tools Enhanced storytelling capacity Strategic mission amplification For organizations seeking growth, awareness, and stronger public engagement, this initiative could become a major catalyst for expansion and long-term impact. Final Thoughts The Bombshell Impact Design Agency Social Impact Grant 2026 stands out as an innovative and mission-driven opportunity for organizations working toward meaningful global change. Rather than focusing solely on financial funding, the initiative addresses one of the biggest modern challenges facing nonprofits and social enterprises: visibility. For organizations with transformative missions but limited branding or marketing capacity, this grant could provide the professional support needed to elevate their message, strengthen their outreach, and expand their influence globally. Interested organizations should begin preparing their applications, impact narratives, and short video submissions before the August 19, 2026 deadline. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026: Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates in Switzerland, China, India, and Japan
summary: Applications Open for the World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026 Key Details Organization: World Economic Forum (WEF) Programme: Early Careers Programme – Fall 2026 Cohort Locations: Geneva (Switzerland), Beijing (China), Mumbai (India), Tokyo (Japan) Duration: 6 months (full-time, onsite) Start Date: Between September 1 and October 1, 2026 Eligibility: Bachelor s or Master s graduates with up to 3 years of cumulative work experience (including internships) Monthly Compensation: Geneva: CHF 4,500 Beijing: CNY 12,500 Mumbai: INR 91,666 Tokyo: JPY 333,333 Work Authorization Support: Available for Geneva-based positions only Application Deadline: June 14, 2026 Are you a recent graduate looking to launch your international career with one of the world s leading organizations? The World Economic Forum (WEF) Early Careers Programme 2026 is now accepting applications for its Fall 2026 cohort. This paid six-month programme offers young professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience while contributing to solutions for some of the world s most pressing global challenges. Successful candidates will be based in Geneva (Switzerland), Beijing (China), Mumbai (India), or Tokyo (Japan) and will work alongside experts, policymakers, business leaders, and innovators from around the world. About the World Economic Forum The World Economic Forum is an independent international organization committed to improving the state of the world through public-private cooperation. Headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, the Forum brings together leaders from government, business, academia, civil society, and international organizations to address global issues and drive collective action. With over 1,000 employees representing more than 100 nationalities, the Forum provides a dynamic and multicultural working environment where young professionals can develop valuable skills and expand their global networks. Programme Details Duration 6-month full-time onsite employment Start Date Between September 1 and October 1, 2026 Locations Geneva, Switzerland Beijing, China Mumbai, India Tokyo, Japan Compensation Participants will receive competitive monthly compensation: Geneva: CHF 4,500 (salary and medical allowance) Beijing: CNY 12,500 Mumbai: INR 91,666 Tokyo: JPY 333,333 Work Authorization Please note that visa and work authorization support is available only for Geneva-based positions. Who Can Apply? The programme is designed for: Bachelor s degree graduates Master s degree graduates Candidates with a maximum of three years of cumulative work experience, including internships This opportunity is ideal for recent graduates seeking international exposure and practical experience within a globally recognized organization. Available Career Areas Participants can join various functional and thematic teams across the organization. Business Development and Engagement Support strategic partnerships and stakeholder engagement initiatives that advance the Forum s mission. Project and Community Management Coordinate collaborative projects and help global communities drive progress on critical challenges. Applicants interested in these areas can select from thematic sectors including: Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains Artificial Intelligence, Frontier Technologies, and Cybersecurity Economic Growth, Equity, Inclusion, Human Capital, and Skills Energy and Materials Financial and Monetary Systems Regional Government Agenda, Trade, and Geopolitics Health and Healthcare Sustainability, Nature, and Climate Urban Transformation Digital and Data Support the digital infrastructure, data systems, and technological tools that enable global collaboration. Events and Experience Help design and deliver world-class conferences, meetings, and digital experiences. Communication and Outreach Create engaging content and communications that showcase the Forum s initiatives and impact. Core Enabling Functions Support the organization s operations through roles in: Finance, Procurement, Legal and Compliance Hospitality and Facility Management People and Culture (Human Resources) Skills You Will Develop Participants will gain valuable professional competencies, including: Global stakeholder engagement Relationship management Strategic communication Problem-solving and critical thinking Project management Adaptability and resilience Professional judgment and decision-making Cross-cultural collaboration Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the Fall 2026 cohort. Selection Process The recruitment process includes several stages: Application submission Initial screening Asynchronous video interview Interviews with hiring managers and team members Personality assessment Talent Acquisition interview Reference and background checks Final offer Why Apply? The World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme offers a unique opportunity to: Gain international work experience Build a global professional network Work on projects with real-world impact Develop highly sought-after professional skills Collaborate with leaders from government, business, and civil society Enhance your career prospects in international development, public policy, business, technology, sustainability, and global affairs For graduates passionate about shaping the future and addressing global challenges, this programme provides an exceptional platform to learn, contribute, and grow. Application Deadline Deadline: June 14, 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the Fall 2026 cohort. The post World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026: Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates in Switzerland, China, India, and Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers
summary: Students interested in software engineering, technology, and innovation now have an exciting opportunity to gain firsthand exposure to one of the world s leading financial institutions. The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 is officially accepting applications from students who are eager to explore careers in engineering, strengthen their technical abilities, and build meaningful professional networks. Designed specifically for students in their penultimate or final year of study, the Engineering Academy is a highly interactive two-day insight program that introduces participants to the world of software engineering at Goldman Sachs. About the Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy is an immersive learning experience aimed at helping students better understand the role of engineering within the organization and the broader technology industry. The program is open to students from all academic disciplines who have an interest in programming, technology, and using logical thinking to build scalable software solutions and systems. Through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and collaborative activities, participants will gain valuable exposure to engineering careers while developing skills that can support future applications for software engineering opportunities. Why Engineering Matters at Goldman Sachs Engineering sits at the heart of Goldman Sachs global operations. The firm s engineers develop innovative technologies that power businesses, financial markets, and client solutions around the world. They combine cutting-edge technologies, open-source software, cloud computing solutions, databases, and programming languages to create applications that are accurate, scalable, and impactful. Software engineers at Goldman Sachs work on complex challenges that require creativity, technical expertise, and collaboration. The Engineering Academy offers participants a chance to learn how these professionals contribute to solving real-world business problems through technology. What Participants Will Gain Students selected for the program will have the opportunity to: 1. Explore Engineering Career Opportunities Participants will discover the wide range of career paths available within software engineering and technology at Goldman Sachs. 2. Develop Technical Skills The program includes practical workshops and collaborative activities designed to strengthen technical knowledge and problem-solving abilities. 3. Build Soft Skills In addition to technical development, students will improve essential professional skills such as: Communication Teamwork Leadership Collaboration Critical thinking Professional networking 4. Learn from Industry Professionals Participants will interact directly with Goldman Sachs professionals and gain insights into their career journeys, experiences, and diverse backgrounds. 5. Expand Professional Networks Students will connect with peers who share similar interests while building relationships with professionals working across engineering and technology teams. 6. Experience Goldman Sachs Culture The program provides valuable insight into Goldman Sachs long-standing apprenticeship culture and commitment to professional growth and excellence. Program Location The 2026 Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy will take place in: Birmingham, United Kingdom The program will run over two days in September 2026. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the Engineering Academy, applicants must: Be enrolled in a Bachelor s or Master s degree program. Be graduating in 2027 or June 2028. Have an interest in software engineering, programming, technology, and problem-solving. Be interested in learning how engineering supports innovation within financial services and technology. Importantly, the program welcomes students from all academic backgrounds, making it accessible to individuals who may not be pursuing traditional computer science or engineering degrees. What Makes This Opportunity Valuable? The Engineering Academy offers more than just a short learning experience. It serves as a platform for students to: Explore future software engineering careers. Gain exposure to a global financial institution. Strengthen both technical and professional competencies. Learn directly from experienced engineers. Build a network of peers and industry professionals. Better understand the skills needed to succeed in technology-focused careers. For students considering a future in software engineering or technology, the program provides a valuable opportunity to gain practical insights before graduation. Application Timeline Application Deadline 21 June 2026 Notification Period Applicants can expect to hear back from Goldman Sachs by July 2026 . Programme Dates The Engineering Academy will take place over two days in September 2026 . How to Apply Interested students should submit their applications through Goldman Sachs Careers. Students are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the deadline. Apply Here Final Thoughts The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for students to gain exposure to software engineering careers, strengthen their technical and professional skills, and connect with industry experts. Whether you come from a technical or non-technical academic background, this programme offers a valuable introduction to the engineering teams that drive innovation across one of the world s most influential organizations. If you are graduating in 2027 or June 2028 and have an interest in technology, software development, and problem-solving, this programme could be an important step toward your future career. For more information on this opportunity visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY, here The post Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher
summary: The South African Research Chair in Violent Histories and Transgenerational Trauma (SARChI Chair), based at the Centre for the Study of the Afterlife of Violence and the Reparative Quest (AVReQ) at Stellenbosch University, has opened applications for its 2027 MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships. Funded by South Africa’s National Research Foundation (NRF), these prestigious scholarships support academically outstanding students who wish to pursue advanced research into the enduring legacies of violence, trauma, memory, healing, and social repair. The programme provides a unique opportunity for emerging scholars to engage with critical questions surrounding violent histories, transgenerational trauma, psychosocial healing, and decolonial knowledge production within a collaborative and interdisciplinary academic environment. About the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair The MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair is a specialized postgraduate programme designed to equip scholars with the theoretical, methodological, and practical tools necessary to examine the complex and long-lasting consequences of violence across generations. The programme focuses on: Decolonial approaches to knowledge production Violent histories and their continuing social impacts Transgenerational trauma and memory Psychosocial processes of healing and repair Social justice and restorative practices Community-based and collaborative research Public engagement and intellectual contribution Students will become part of the vibrant intellectual community at AVReQ, engaging with scholars, researchers, and practitioners working on some of the most pressing questions related to violence, justice, memory, and healing. Scholarship Benefits The scholarship supports full-time enrolment in the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair programme at Stellenbosch University. Scholarship Duration Two years of funded study Programme Start Date January 2027 Funding Structure South African citizens and permanent residents may receive full NRF scholarships. International students who are selected may receive partial scholarship funding. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Academic Requirements Applicants must: Hold an Honours degree or equivalent qualification before the MPhil programme begins. Have achieved a minimum final mark of 65% in their Honours degree. Have completed their Honours degree no earlier than 2024. Be able to provide proof of graduation or confirmation from a supervisor indicating expected graduation by the end of the 2026 academic year. Research and Personal Commitment Applicants must also demonstrate: A strong commitment to postgraduate education and research. A genuine interest in exploring issues related to violent histories, trauma, memory, justice, healing, and repair. A willingness to contribute actively to a collaborative research community. Intellectual curiosity and engagement with decolonial scholarship. Required Application Documents Applicants must first apply for admission to the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair through Stellenbosch University s online application system. In addition, candidates must submit a separate scholarship application by email. The scholarship application package must include: 1. Academic Qualification Documents Copy of Honours degree certificate, OR Letter from a supervisor confirming expected graduation by the end of 2026 2. Sample of Academic Work Applicants should provide one example of previous academic writing, such as: Academic essay Journal publication Dissertation chapter Honours research project chapter 3. Scholarship Motivation Letter (Maximum Three Pages) The motivation letter should address: Academic background and achievements Leadership experience and community engagement Professional, civic, or academic involvement Research interests and alignment with the programme Intended contribution to AVReQ s intellectual and public life Financial need (where applicable) 4. Recommendation Letter Applicants must submit: At least one recommendation letter from an academic mentor familiar with their work and capabilities 5. Declaration Form Candidates must complete and submit: Declaration of Authorship, Generative AI Use, and Writing Assistance Form Submission Process All scholarship materials should be submitted via email to: AVREQINFO@sun.ac.za Use the following email subject line: MPHIL SCHOLARSHIP APPLICATION 2027 Application Deadline All applications must be received by: Monday, 22 June 2026 at 12:00 PM (Noon) Late applications are unlikely to be considered. Interview Process Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in interviews. Interview Date 25 June 2026 Applicants should ensure they are available on this date if shortlisted. Why Apply? This scholarship offers a rare opportunity to study one of the most urgent and relevant areas of contemporary scholarship. Successful applicants will benefit from: NRF-funded postgraduate support Access to leading scholars and researchers Participation in a dynamic interdisciplinary research community Training in decolonial and reparative scholarship Opportunities to contribute to public discourse and social transformation Engagement with critical questions surrounding trauma, healing, justice, and memory For students committed to understanding and addressing the enduring effects of violence and historical injustice, the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair provides an exceptional platform for academic growth, research excellence, and meaningful societal impact. For more information on this scholarship visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here The post Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Dalberg Research Fellow (12-Month Fellowship): Global Development Research Opportunity in India with Remote/Hybrid Flexibility
summary: The Dalberg Research Fellow 2026 is a prestigious 12-month fellowship offered by Dalberg Advisors , a globally recognized impact consulting firm working at the intersection of strategy, evidence, design, and systems change. This opportunity is designed for experienced researchers, policy professionals, and development practitioners who want to shape the future of global development through high-impact research, thought leadership, and innovation. Based primarily in India , with remote and hybrid working options available , this fellowship provides a rare opportunity to contribute to a newly emerging research and thought leadership initiative within Dalberg focused on shaping global development agendas. The fellowship is part of Dalberg’s effort to establish a forward-looking research engine that generates insights on major global challenges such as climate finance, gender equity, education systems, AI in development, and inclusive economic growth. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis , with a preferred start date from June 2026 onward . About Dalberg and Its Global Mission Dalberg Advisors is part of the broader Dalberg Group, a global organization committed to building a more inclusive and sustainable world. With 28 offices worldwide and experience in over 135 countries , Dalberg integrates: Strategic advisory expertise Rigorous research and data analysis Deep local and regional knowledge Cross-sector collaboration with governments, NGOs, and private sector actors Dalberg operates at the intersection of: Strategy and policy design Evidence-based development research Systems transformation and innovation Global impact consulting The organization works across major global development themes including education, gender equity, climate change, digital infrastructure, and economic justice. About the Research Thought Leadership Initiative The Research Fellow will contribute to a newly established Dalberg Observatory-style initiative , designed to produce forward-thinking research that shapes global development discourse. This initiative focuses on generating research and insights in emerging global areas such as: Future of outcomes-based funding and philanthropic capital flows Systems change measurement and collective impact evaluation Gender equity challenges, including boys’ education and underexplored inequalities Artificial intelligence in education, work, and public services Future of development financing and global policy innovation The initiative aims to produce high-quality research publications, convenings, and thought leadership that influence policymakers, donors, and global institutions. Key Objectives of the Research Fellow Role The Research Fellow will play a central role in shaping and executing Dalberg’s research agenda by: Leading or supporting original research on global development themes Contributing to quarterly publications and research outputs Translating internal consulting insights into global thought leadership Developing policy-relevant research products and analytical frameworks Supporting experimental research design and foresight analysis Key thematic focus areas include: Global development funding flows (climate, education, gender) Systems change metrics and impact measurement frameworks Education equity, especially boys’ education AI applications in governance, public services, and labor markets Key Responsibilities The Research Fellow will engage in a wide range of analytical and strategic tasks, including: 1. Research and Analysis Conduct structured desk research and literature reviews Perform qualitative and quantitative analysis Build comparative case studies across regions and sectors Support data synthesis and interpretation 2. Writing and Publication Draft research briefs, memos, blogs, and analytical papers Develop thought leadership content for internal and external audiences Contribute to quarterly publications and research outputs 3. Data Visualization and Insight Generation Support development of data visualization products Translate complex datasets into accessible insights Assist in producing research presentations and reports 4. Stakeholder Engagement and Collaboration Engage with internal Dalberg teams globally Support research partnerships and roundtable discussions Assist in designing events and convenings on key topics 5. Institutional Development Contribute to shaping the research initiative’s structure Support operational design and strategic development Help define long-term research direction and outputs Desired Qualifications and Experience Candidates are expected to bring a strong mix of analytical ability, research experience, and intellectual curiosity. Required Qualifications 4–8 years of experience in global development, research, or public policy Master’s degree preferred in fields such as: Public Policy International Affairs Sociology Public Health Data Journalism or related disciplines Technical Skills Strong writing, synthesis, and communication skills Ability to translate complex ideas into accessible insights Experience with at least one statistical tool: Python R Stata SPSS Optional but valuable: GIS mapping tools Qualitative analysis software (NVivo, ATLAS.ti, MAXQDA) Professional Experience Experience in think tanks, research institutes, or policy organizations Familiarity with development finance or global policy systems Exposure to consulting or advisory environments is an advantage Personal Attributes Strong intellectual curiosity and independent thinking Ability to work in ambiguous and evolving research environments Capacity to produce rapid, high-quality research outputs Self-motivated and impact-driven mindset What This Fellowship Offers The Dalberg Research Fellow role is designed as both a professional development opportunity and a potential long-term career pathway. Participants will benefit from: Opportunity to publish high-impact global development research Mentorship from senior global experts Exposure to international policy ecosystems Access to high-level development networks Participation in shaping a new research institution within Dalberg Possibility of transitioning into a long-term leadership role High-trust environment focused on innovation and intellectual freedom This fellowship is ideal for professionals seeking to move into high-level research leadership, policy advisory roles, or global development strategy positions. Application Timeline Applications: Rolling basis Preferred Start Date: From June 2026 Duration: 12 months (full-time fellowship) Location: India (Remote/Hybrid options available) Early application is strongly encouraged due to limited placement capacity and continuous review. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply directly through Dalberg Advisors’ official careers or recruitment channels , following instructions for the Research Fellow position. Apply Here Why This Opportunity Matters The Dalberg Research Fellow role is more than a fellowship—it is a launchpad into the future of global development thinking. It offers participants the chance to influence how governments, donors, and global institutions approach complex challenges such as inequality, climate change, education reform, and digital transformation. For researchers ready to move beyond traditional analysis and contribute to systems-level change, this fellowship provides a rare opportunity to shape global narratives and policy innovation at scale.
